meta.tagline: Curated knowledge for family, learning, and development

items.title: Stop Drowning in AI Overload (5 AI Prompts)
items.excerpt: You read about AI more than you use it. These 5 prompts build a system that filters the noise so + you can do actual work.
items.summary: A productivity guide offers five AI prompts designed to help users filter through excessive AI information and focus on practical applications. The system aims to reduce time spent reading about AI tools and increase actual productive use of the technology.
items.pullQuote: Build a system that filters AI noise for actual work

items.title: 5 Powerful AI Prompts to Build Personal and Business Intelligence Documents for Every AI Tool (Beginner Friendly)
items.excerpt: Stop re-explaining yourself to every AI tool. Build personal and business intelligence documents that carry your context across Claude, Perplexity, Gemini, ChatGPT, local LLMs, agents, and automated workflows.
items.summary: A guide demonstrates how to create standardized personal and business intelligence documents that maintain context across multiple AI platforms. The approach eliminates the need to repeatedly explain personal circumstances to different AI tools like ChatGPT, Claude, and Gemini.
items.pullQuote: Stop re-explaining yourself to every AI tool with intelligence documents

items.title: Minor League Transactions: Feb. 25–March 15, 2026
items.excerpt: A summary of minor league moves over the past three weeks. The post Minor League Transactions: Feb. 25–March 15, 2026 appeared first on College Baseball, MLB Draft, Prospects - Baseball America.
items.summary: Baseball America compiled minor league player moves and roster changes over a three-week period in spring 2026. The transactions provide insight into organizational depth and player movement patterns across professional baseball's development system.
items.pullQuote: Minor league moves reveal organizational depth and development patterns

items.title: Are College Baseball Teams Making A Mistake By Ignoring Soft-Tossing Pitchers?
items.excerpt: J.J. Cooper digs into college pitching data to see how players with slower fastballs compare to harder throwers. The post Are College Baseball Teams Making A Mistake By Ignoring Soft-Tossing Pitchers? appeared first on College Baseball, MLB Draft, Prospects - Baseball America.
items.summary: Data analysis reveals college teams may be overlooking effective pitchers who throw with lower velocity but achieve strong results through command and movement. The research challenges the modern emphasis on pure fastball speed in recruiting and development.
items.pullQuote: Effective pitchers with lower velocity achieve results through command and movement

items.title: Lawsuit Accuses Breaking Platform Whatnot Of Being 'Unregulated Online Casino' For Sports Cards (Report)
items.excerpt: A total of 15 arbitration complaints have been filed against Whatnot, per the Athletic, alleging the company's practices represent illegal gambling. The post Lawsuit Accuses Breaking Platform Whatnot Of Being ‘Unregulated Online Casino’ For Sports Cards (Report) appeared first on College Baseball, MLB Draft, Prospects - Baseball America.
items.summary: Fifteen arbitration complaints filed against sports card platform Whatnot allege the company operates as an illegal gambling enterprise. The lawsuits target the platform's card-breaking practices and their similarity to casino operations.
items.pullQuote: Sports card platform faces gambling allegations in multiple lawsuits

items.title: Scouting Every NL East No. 1 Prospect For 2026

items.excerpt: Can the world still avoid an energy calamity?

items.title: Kagi Small Web

items.title: Grace Hopper's Revenge
items.excerpt: Article URL: https://www.thefuriousopposites.com/p/grace-hoppers-revenge Comments URL: https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=47410349 Points: 12 # Comments: 6
items.summary: Historical analysis explores computing pioneer Grace Hopper's contributions and their relevance to modern software development challenges. The piece examines how her early insights about programming abstraction and compiler design vindicate her approach decades later.
items.pullQuote: Programming abstraction insights vindicate her approach decades later

items.title: Trump's War Psychology
items.excerpt: An extension of id by other means.
items.summary: Analysis examines Trump's psychological approach to military conflict and foreign policy decision-making regarding Iran. The piece explores how personal impulses may drive strategic military choices.
items.pullQuote: Extension of id by other means shapes military strategy

items.title: 'Evacuation Day' and the American Character
items.excerpt: How the British occupation of Boston—which ended 250 years ago today—shaped our political sensibility.
items.summary: Historical analysis examines how the British evacuation of Boston 250 years ago influenced fundamental American political attitudes and values. The piece connects colonial resistance to modern political sensibilities.
items.pullQuote: British occupation of Boston shaped our political sensibility

items.title: Iran Gets a Vote in This War
items.excerpt: The administration doesn’t seem to have planned for that.
items.summary: Strategic analysis argues the Trump administration lacks comprehensive planning for Iranian responses to military escalation. The piece examines potential Iranian countermoves and strategic vulnerabilities.
items.pullQuote: Administration doesn't seem to have planned for Iranian responses

items.title: Distracted Trump Rambles While Iran War Expands

items.summary: Commentary analyzes Trump's recent Oval Office statements about Iran amid escalating military tensions. Political analysts discuss the disconnect between presidential communication and strategic coherence.
items.pullQuote: Trump rambles while Iran war expands globally

items.title: Sen. Kennedy Tried to Humiliate This Witness… It Backfired (w/ David Bier)
items.excerpt: Tim Miller talks with Cato’s David Bier about his viral clash with Senator John Kennedy—and what the exchange reveals about today’s immigration debate.
items.summary: Immigration policy expert David Bier discusses his confrontational Senate hearing exchange with Senator John Kennedy that went viral. The conversation explores broader dynamics in contemporary immigration policy debates.
items.pullQuote: Viral clash reveals today's immigration debate dynamics

briefing.editorsNote: Today's stories remind us that wisdom often lies in questioning conventional thinking and learning from overlooked perspectives.

bullets.text: Learn to create reusable AI context documents that work across ChatGPT, Claude, and Gemini platforms.
College scouts may be missing effective pitchers by prioritizing velocity over command and results.
Grace Hopper's compiler innovations from decades ago are proving prescient for today's software challenges.
Boston's 250-year-old Evacuation Day reveals enduring patterns in American political character and resistance.
Bitcoin continues gaining value as investors hedge against geopolitical tensions and economic uncertainty.
